reasoning: The informal statement asks to exhibit a bijection between the collection of open subsets 𝒯 and the collection of closed subsets 𝒦 of a metric space M. The Lean statement passes O, C : Set (Set M) with the hypotheses O = {s | IsOpen s} and C = {s | IsClosed s}, and asks for ∃ f : O → C, Bijective f. Here O and C coerce to subtypes {s : Set M // IsOpen s} and {s : Set M // IsClosed s}, and Bijective f is the standard (Injective ∧ Surjective) notion, which is exactly what a bijection between the two collections means. The natural witness (complement) gives such a bijection, matching the intended content. No hypotheses are added or dropped, and the quantifier/conclusion structure lines up.
